teachings.teaching: The one described is despised and rejected by others, portraying how suffering can lead to social dismissal.
Great pain and grief can be central to a person’s experience (“man of sorrows … familiar with sickness”), emphasizing the reality of suffering.
People may hide their faces from someone in pain, reflecting avoidance, shame, or unwillingness to engage with hardship.
There can be a failure to recognize the true worth or significance of the suffering person (“and we did not esteem him”).

goals.goal: To depict and explain the rejection of a suffering figure (commonly understood as a messianic figure) and to highlight how others misjudge and undervalue him, setting up a deeper call to recognize his meaning.
